quiz_questions.question_text: The shortest distance from a point to a line is measured along:
quiz_questions.explanation: The shortest distance from a Point to a Line is always along the perpendicular segment. Distance Between Parallel Lines The distance between two parallel lines is the length of the perpendicular segment between them. The other options — 'Any segment to the line', 'A diagonal line', and 'A parallel line' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

quiz_questions.question_text: The distance between parallel lines is:
quiz_questions.explanation: This distance is constant everywhere. Distance Between Parallel Lines The distance between two parallel lines is the length of the perpendicular segment between them. The other options — 'Different at every point', 'Zero', and 'Undefined' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

quiz_questions.question_text: A perpendicular bisector passes through the:
quiz_questions.explanation: Perpendicular Bisector A perpendicular bisector of a segment is a Line perpendicular to the segment at its midpoint. The shortest distance from a Point to a Line is always along the perpendicular segment. The other options — 'Endpoint of a segment', 'Vertex of an angle', and 'Center of a circle' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

quiz_questions.question_text: Any point on the perpendicular bisector is:
quiz_questions.explanation: Any Point on the perpendicular bisector is equidistant from the endpoints. The shortest distance from a Point to a Line is always along the perpendicular segment. The other options — 'Closer to one endpoint', 'On the segment itself', and 'At the midpoint' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

quiz_questions.question_text: The distance from point P to line l is measured along what?

options.text: The segment from P perpendicular to l
The longest segment from P to l
Any line from P to l

quiz_questions.explanation: Distance from a Point to a Line The distance from a Point to a Line is measured along the perpendicular segment from the Point to the Line. The shortest distance from a Point to a Line is always along the perpendicular segment. The other options — 'The longest segment from P to l', 'Any line from P to l', and 'A parallel line through P' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

quiz_questions.question_text: If two parallel lines are 5 units apart, every perpendicular segment between them measures:
quiz_questions.explanation: Distance Between Parallel Lines The distance between two parallel lines is the length of the perpendicular segment between them. Perpendicular Lines Two lines are perpendicular if they intersect at right angles (90°). The other options — 'More than 5 units', 'Less than 5 units', and 'It varies' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

Perpendicular Lines Two lines are perpendicular if they intersect at right angles (90°). Distance Between Parallel Lines The distance between two parallel lines is the length of the perpendicular segment between them. The other options — '45°', '180°', and '60°' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
